title: Resume V2
description: You will create a polished resume suitable for a high school that showcases your academic achievements, experiences, and skills. This resume will help you organize your qualifications into a coherent story for potential employers or internship coordinators. By the end of this project, you'll have a polished document that represents who you are and where you're headed professionally.

sections.title: File Setup
sections.description: Set up your resume structure by creating the main section headings and updating your file name. Focus on proper heading formatting and document organization.

objectives.title: Headings
objectives.description:
Leave line #1 blank. Then insert five section headings in the following order. You must style as heading3 and capitalize the first letter. Watch the task video(s) for step-by-step instructions and common errors.

  1. Summary
  2. Education
  3. Experience
  4. Skills
  5. You pick this one.
  6. Leadership / Extracurriculars / Projects / Certifications / Volunteer / Achievements / Languages

resources.title: Video1.1
resources.transcript_prompt: Review the transcript, then summarize it for high school students. Start with; This video is a tutorial on how to ......

objectives.title: File Name
objectives.description:
Rename your file using this naming convention:

Last, First | Assignment Name | Period


Example:

Benrud, Todd | Resume | Period #5

sections.title: Contact Information
sections.description:
Include the following information.

  1. Your full name.
  2. Your phone number.
  3. Your school email address (One associated with your GradeFlow account)
  4. Your city and state in the City, State format (Capitalized Abbreviation)


EXAMPLE:

<phone> | <email> | <location>

(234) 567-8190 | email@address.com | San Diego, CA

objectives.title: Your Name
objectives.description: Add your full name to the first line of your Google Doc. Capitalize your First and Last name. The font must be 20 sizes or larger and bold.

resources.title: Video2.1
resources.transcript_prompt: Review the transcript, then summarize it for high school students. Start with; This video is a tutorial on how to ......

objectives.title: Contact Details
objectives.description:
Add you phone number, email address, and location. Separate each element with a delimiter like a pipe, slash, etc....

  1. Phone number must use the Common Display Format for North American, 3-3-4 split for the 10 digits to maintain readability.
  2. Email address must be hyperlinked and be the one associated with your GradeFlow profile.
  3. Location must be in the City, State format. State must be the official 2-letter abbreviation.


EXAMPLE:

  1. (123) 456-7890 / email@address.com / City, ST

resources.title: Video2.2
resources.transcript_prompt: Review the transcript, then summarize it for high school students. Start with; This video is a tutorial on how to ......

sections.title: Summary Section
sections.description:
Write a clear and concise summary statement that tells employers who you are, including your grade level, a standout trait, and your intended path or interest. Name one or two strengths or skills that match the role you want. Add one proof point, such as your GPA, a certification, a project, an award, or a measurable result. Keep it specific, include numbers when you can, tailor it to the role, and avoid buzzwords, fluff, and long lists. Aim for 1 to 2 sentences, roughly 20 - 30 words.


EXAMPLES:

  1. Dependable junior seeking part-time retail role; 3.6 GPA; school store cashier handling $500 daily. Available evenings and weekends.
  2. STEM-focused senior seeking data internship; Python and Excel; built a survey dashboard used by 60 students that improved planning decisions.
  3. Motivated senior seeking peer tutor role; AP Calculus score 5; raised tutees’ quiz averages by 12 percent through targeted review.

objectives.title: Insert Section: {{var:OBJECTIVE}}
objectives.description: Insert a horizontal line and Insert a Section Break (continuous) before the "{{var:OBJECTIVE}}" heading3. Both must be styled as "normal". Watch the task video(s) for step-by-step instructions and common errors.

objectives.title: Write Summary Section
objectives.description: Write a clear and concise summary statement between the "SUMMARY" and "EDUCATION" headings. It must be between 10 and 25 words in length.

resources.title: Video3.2
resources.transcript_prompt: Review the transcript, then summarize it for high school students. Start with; This video is a tutorial on how to ......

sections.title: Education Section
sections.description:
Insert a Section Break (continous) before the "EDUCATION" heading. Then write a clear and concise objective statement that is between 10 and 25 words in length. Your objective statement must be between "EDUCATION" and "EXPERIENCE" headings.


  1. The name of your school (most recent first)
  2. Expected graduation date (month and year)
  3. GPA (if it's above 3.0)
  4. Relevant coursework (optional, but only include if it's related to the job you're applying for)




As a high school student, education is likely your most important section. It shows your academic background and whether you’re a strong student.

  1. The name of your school (most recent first)
  2. Expected graduation date (month and year)
  3. GPA (if it's above 3.0)
  4. Relevant coursework (optional, but only include if it's related to the job you're applying for)

objectives.title: High School
objectives.description:
Add the following to your "{{var:EDUCATION}}" section.

  1. Your high school's full name.
  2. Location in the City, State format
  3. Year attended in the four-digit year hyphen four-digit format
  4. 2020-2024
  5. Two bullet points:
  6. The first, relavant courses and/or projects related to the type of work you are interested in.
  7. The second, your GPA in the one decimal format.
  8. GPA: 3.5

resources.transcript_prompt: Review the transcript, then summarize it for high school students. Start with; This video is a tutorial on how to ......

sections.title: Experience Section
sections.description:
Employers want to know about the kind of work or activities you have experience with. Even if they are not directly related to the job you're applying for. It shows you have a responsibility and a work ethic.


Jobs, internships, volunteer work, sports, clubs, etc... (most recent first). For each, include the company/organization name, your role, location, and dates. Add 2-3 bullet points for each role that explain your tasks and responsibilities.

objectives.title: Insert Section: {{var:EXPERIENCE}}
objectives.description: Insert a horizontal line and Insert a Section Break (continuous) before the "{{var:EXPERIENCE}}" heading3. Both must be styled as "normal". Watch the task video(s) for step-by-step instructions and common errors.

resources.title: Video5.1
resources.transcript_prompt: Review the transcript, then summarize it for high school students. Start with; This video is a tutorial on how to ......

objectives.title: Experience #1
objectives.description:
Add the following to your "{{var:EXPERIENCE}}" section.

  1. A role or position you had that provided you with some type of experience.
  2. After the role, include the word 'at' then include the name of the company, organization, etc...
  3. Location in the City, State format
  4. Years of experience in the four-digit year hyphen four-digit format
  5. EXAMPLE: 2020-2024
  6. At least one bullet point:
  7. Describe your responsibilities in a clear, concise statement.

objectives.title: Experience #2
objectives.description:
Add a 2nd experience.

  1. A role or position you had that provided you with some type of experience.
  2. Location in the City, State format
  3. Years of experience in the four-digit year hyphen four-digit format
  4. EXAMPLE: 2020-2024
  5. At least one bullet point:
  6. Describe your responsibilities in a clear, concise statement.

sections.title: Skills Section
sections.description: Create a list of 5 to 10 skills that match your target roles. Include both technical skills, such as Google Workspace or beginner coding, and workplace skills, such as communication, teamwork, leadership, or time management. Group similar skills so they are easy to scan, avoid repeating the same idea with different words, and keep capitalization consistent. Use Evaluate to verify coverage and readability.

objectives.title: Table
objectives.description:
Watch the task video(s) for step-by-step instructions and common errors.

  1. Insert a 1-row 2-column table below the "{{var:SKILLS}}" heading3.
  2. Add at least six skills, one bullet point per skill, three skills per column.
  3. Bullets should be one to three words, not sentences or long statements.

sections.title: Custom Section
sections.description: Create a short list of 10 to 20 skills that match your target roles. Include both technical skills, such as Google Workspace or beginner coding, and workplace skills, such as communication, teamwork, leadership, or time management. Group similar skills so they are easy to scan, avoid repeating the same idea with different words, and keep capitalization consistent. Use Evaluate to verify coverage and readability.

objectives.title: Insert Section: Custom
objectives.description: Insert a horizontal line and Insert a Section Break (continuous) before your custom heading3. Both must be styled as "normal". Watch the task video(s) for step-by-step instructions and common errors.

resources.title: Video5.1
resources.transcript_prompt: Review the transcript, then summarize it for high school students. Start with; This video is a tutorial on how to ......

objectives.title: Custom Section Content
objectives.description:
Watch the task video(s) for step-by-step instructions and common errors.

  1. Use AI and/or the internet to learn about what belongs in your custom section.
  2. Using only text and bullets, construct your custom section.
  3. It must consist of at least 20 words and 2 bullet points.

sections.title: Teacher Grader
sections.description: Your teacher will evaluate your completed resume to ensure each section contains accurate, well-organized information that effectively presents your qualifications. Double-check each of the tasks below to ensure you submit high quality work.

objectives.title: Summary and Formatting
objectives.description: Review your Summary section. Verify it includes all required elements: your current grade level, one standout trait or strength, one or two relevant skills, and one concrete achievement with numbers (GPA, certification, or measurable result). Your statement should be 20-30 words, avoid generic buzzwords, and maintain a professional tone.

data.user_prompt:
`Grading Criteria`

1) Evaluate if the Summary section contains a clear statement (1-2 sentences, 20-40 words) that includes: grade level or expected graduation year, a standout trait or characteristic, two to three relevant strengths or skills that match the student's intended role, and one concrete proof point with numbers when possible (such as GPA, certification, measurable result, project, or achievement). Verify the statement avoids generic buzzwords like "hardworking" or "team player" and maintains specific, concrete language tailored to the student's career interest or target role. Verify the statement flows logically, communicates the student's value clearly, and maintains a professional tone appropriate for high school students entering the workforce.

2) Evaluate if the resume maintains consistent formatting throughout the entire document, including uniform font choices, standardized bullet point styles, even margins, and balanced spacing between sections. Verify that all section headings are clearly labeled, visually distinct through bold text or capitalization, and formatted consistently across the document. Check that the resume uses bullet points appropriately to break down details in the Experience section, making information easy to read and scan quickly. The resume should present a simple, clean, and organized appearance that demonstrates attention to detail suitable for high school students seeking employment or internships.

3) Contact information is center aligned, sections are tabled, a horizontal line indicated the end of each section (excluding the last one), and the table borders are invisible.

objectives.title: Education and Experience
objectives.description:
Review your Summary section. Verify it includes all required elements: your current grade level, one standout trait or strength, one or two relevant skills, and one concrete achievement with numbers (GPA, certification, or measurable result). Your statement should be 20-30 words, avoid generic buzzwords, and maintain a professional tone.


Review your Education section. Verify it includes your school name, years attended, and location in City, State format. Confirm you have two bullet points: one describing your coursework or academics, and one stating your GPA in X.X format (e.g., "GPA: 3.5").

data.user_prompt:
`Grading Criteria`

1) Evaluate if the Education section contains required elements: school name, years attended, and location formatted as City, State abbreviation. Each education institution must include two bullet points. Bullet point one must describe that student's coursework or academics. Bullet point one must must state GPA in X.X format (e.g., "GPA: 3.5").

objectives.title: Skills
objectives.description:
Review your Skills section. Verify you have a properly formatted 1-row, 2-column table positioned directly below the "Skills" heading. Confirm the table includes at least six skills total with three skills per column, each as a separate bullet point. Check that each skill is concise (1-3 words only), uses professional terminology, and avoids complete sentences or lengthy descriptions.


Review your custom section. Verify you selected one allowed heading (Leadership, Extracurriculars, Projects, Certifications, Volunteer, Achievements, or Languages). Confirm your section contains at least 20 words total and includes at least one bullet point. Check that your content is relevant to the section type you chose, demonstrates appropriate research, and uses only text and bullets (no tables, graphics, or special formatting).

data.user_prompt:
`Grading Criteria`

1) Evaluate if the Skills section contains a properly formatted 1-row, 2-column table positioned directly below the "Skills" heading. Verify the table includes at least six skills total with three skills per column, each as a separate bullet point. Check that each skill entry is concise (1-3 words only), uses professional terminology relevant to high school students, and avoids complete sentences or lengthy descriptions.

2) Evaluate if the student selected one allowed custom section heading (Leadership, Extracurriculars, Projects, Certifications, Volunteer, Achievements, or Languages). Verify the section contains at least 20 words total and includes at least one bullet point. Check that content is relevant to the chosen section type, demonstrates research into appropriate section content, and uses only text and bullets (no tables, graphics, or special formatting).

Section-Specific Expectations:
Leadership: Positions held, organizations led, team sizes, initiatives implemented
Extracurriculars: Club names, roles, years participated, key activities or contributions
Projects: Project titles, dates, descriptions, outcomes or technologies used
Certifications: Certification names, issuing organizations, dates earned
Volunteer: Organization names, roles, hours served, impact or responsibilities
Achievements: Award names, competitions, dates, level of recognition (school/regional/national)
Languages: Language names, proficiency levels, relevant experience or certifications
